entry.title: Re: 銀行媒體檔(Map Format Data)格式，明細行流水號設置
entry.content:
Hi Rainie,

You should

  1. create a Numeric variable in Format Mapping Properties (Initial value can be 0, but does not have to be mentioned)
  2. create a Calculation Node in the segment (as a first node in the segment)
  3. in the Calculation Node increase the value of the variable by 1 (so it will be increased for each line)
  4. add an element behind the calculation node
  5. set the element appropriately (mapping procedure is Variable and so on)

Best regards,

Antonin

entry.title: Confirm alerts in SAP Cloud ALM using SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Dear integration experts,

Monolith systems are almost extinct and today IT landscapes are composed for multiple SAP and non-SAP applications that need to be inter connected. The need to govern and monitor all these integrations is crucial for successfully running each company business processes. In the SAP domain, SAP Cloud ALM (CALM) is one of the options, if not the best, to monitor different services and systems of your SAP Landscape, including of course the SAP integration tools, SAP Integration Suite or Managed Gateway for Spend & Network (former CIG) and soon Advance Event Mesh:

 

Plugging in these tools in the Integration & Exception Monitoring or the Health Monitoring gives already great insights of what is going on with your system:

However, how to process the alerts will make the difference. You can wait for the business to complain or react immediately to the alerts triggered. 

SAP Cloud ALM offers a wide range of Event actions:

  1. Send email: Oldie but goldie. 
  2. Send chat notification: I would say email 2.0
  3. Create ticket: Much better governance than the ones before. Usually this was a problem if you had timeout issues, and many messages failed for the same reason creating hundreds of tickets, but now you can group the events to not trigger more alerts of the same type until the first is confirmed.
  4. Start Operation flow: I would like to focus on this one as it opens the doors for endless possibilities. 

The operation flows can have different flavors:

 

Unfortunately, I miss here a fifth option to send directly the event to SAP Integration Suite, so I would like to explore the usage of SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) to process the event triggered by an alert in CALM.

This page explain the initial steps you need to follow to be able to link a process from BPA to an event action in CALM.

Once that is done and your process in BPA is created, you can go to the Operations Automation app of CALM:

And register a new operation flow for BPA. Selecting the destination to BPA you can select the Processes that are deployed in the Public environment of BPA.

Click OK:

And now in the System/Service configuration, you can navigate to the events and add the event action to trigger the process automation:

Use case for our Process Automation

This is the best part, as you have a white canvas to do whatever is best for you.  As every organization have their own tools, politics, dynamics (good or bad), etc... not every solution will fit for all. 

What I was exploring is to auto-confirm the alerts for CIG, sorry, Managed Gateway for Spend & Network 🙄, because the alerts are sent by email to the team responsible, but they don't have access to Cloud ALM to confirm them when they are resolved, making it look bad (or unmanaged) to the admin team of CALM.

As a disclaimer, I must admit I am not an expert either in CALM or BPA, so this is just based on my experience playing around, and it might be a better/easier way to achieve the same.

First of all, we need to know the possibilities offered by CALM to close the alerts remotely. Once again, SAP Business Accelerator Hub is the way to go to find an API that can fulfill our needs. Please check as well the help page.

Please note that we are going to close the event situation and that will close the alert and not close the alert directly. This will be important because the IDs of the event situation and the alert are not the same.

As a pre-requisite you will need to create a service instance in the CALM subaccount and create a service key. Please follow this steps.  The scope required is calm-api.event-situations.write.

Before diving into the Build Process, it is always helpful to test manually using your preferred HTTP client. 

If everything goes well you will get the next response:

And you might be thinking, how do I get the Event Situation ID from CALM? But unfortunately I do not have the answer. I could not find it anywhere. Ideally, it should be here:

But the ID is not displayed (or I could not find it) so the only way I had to find it was to create the process in BPA and generate an alert. Then I could see the event in the Monitoring section...

Let's dive then in the Build Process Automation, basically we need two things:

  1. Receive the event
  2. Extract the id to pass it to the API call back.

For me, it would make sense to import the event as a connector from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub but the event is not available 😕  so we can use an API trigger inside the process.

For the second part, we can create an Action importing the API from the Business Accelerator Hub:

And you will get all  the content available:

Input

Output

But the output body is empty! And we have seen in Postman that the body response contains some data, so we need to add it, otherwise the BPA process will throw an error even though the call to CALM is successful. However, adding the body structure can be done using a Sample JSON:

And that will create the needed structure:

Now, you can create the Process:

Which will be quite simple:

In the Process Details, we need to add just one field called id (type string), because luckily it is not nested within any other field in the event structure.

And the result:

The API trigger is even simpler:

For the last step, we need to select the destination pointing to CALM:

And map the if field, and the state.

The output expected will now match with the API response:

And that's it. Save, Release and Deploy in the public environment!

Next time your alerts will be closed automatically and the issue will disappear! 🤣 I am kidding of course, as someone is still receiving the email and checking what is going on.

Let's review what will happen:

1.- An alert is triggered (because now the alerts are closed almost instantly I am using an old screenshot)

2.- An event is triggered with State: "Open"

3.- The process in BPA is triggered and completed:

And the event is closed automatically:

And the alert will be gone for good. This is not ideal and it would be nice to check alerts closed, specially if someone adds comments on them (not in this use case obviously as it is closed in seconds)

For this I opened a Influence Request: Confirmed alerts in CloudALM visible in CloudALM

Please have a look and vote if you think it is a good idea.

Conclusion

My intention with this small PoC was to do the ground work for future use cases. Having already the integration established between CALM and BPA will low the barrier for the next implementations as the next developers will need to focus only on the process automation part.

What other use cases I could envision?

The simpler one is to decide based on the content of the event which alerts close automatically because they are not relevant instead of all.

Another one could be to send a task to the alert responder to include some details before closing it like what was the root cause, if he/she had to do some action, inform someone, etc... and collect this information in a Hana Database using the vector engine. Then, create a chatbot (will call it Chat Norris 🤠) that can help the team members to find out past solutions for similar alerts.

I would love to hear other ideas in the comments!

Best regards

Javier

entry.title: Re: How to reset system user password in HANA database?
entry.content: Thank you. Its really Informative.

entry.title: Build Once, Reuse Often – New Features in Industrial Systems with SAP Asset Performance Management
entry.content:
An industrial system represents the structure of assets, functions, and relationships that mirror how equipment is organized and managed in real operations. It’s the digital skeleton of your plant or factory, and with 2505, building that skeleton just got smarter. 

Let’s look at the enhancements shaping how we build, manage, and reuse these systems. 

Manage Industrial System Templates – Build it once, reuse it everywhere 

The new Manage Industrial System Templates app lets you define reusable blueprints of a complete system, including: 

  • Multiple aspects and top nodes 
  • System hierarchy and internal relationships 

For the reliability engineer, this means no more reinventing the wheel. Instead of re-building similar structures from scratch, teams can start from a consistent, approved model—ensuring alignment and reducing setup time. 

For more information, see SAP Help Portal Manage Industrial System Templates 

 

 

 

Create Systems from Templates – Fast-track system setup

Once a template is finalized, it becomes a launchpad. The ability to create new industrial systems from templates streamlines the process, reducing complexity and human error—especially across multiple plants or projects. 

Our engineer can now focus on what matters—validating data, not building models. 

For more information, see SAP Help Portal Creating an Industrial System from Template 

 

 

Enhanced System Hierarchy View – Clarity at scale 

Large systems often mean getting lost in the details. The new tabular hierarchy view complements the graphical model, giving teams a more structured overview. Each system component is visible at a glance in a clear table format, helping with reviews, audits, and collaboration. 

A new Aspect Type filter also allows users to narrow down the view—focusing only on relevant components for a given task or analysis. 

 

For more information, see the SAP Help Portal Manage Industrial Systems

Together, the 2505 enhancements simplify system design, save time, drive consistent modeling through reusable templates, and improve system navigation. There are many exciting developments coming in the area of industrial systems—including the ability to create standalone recommendations on industrial systems, helping teams respond quickly to failures in functional structures. A dedicated Meet the Expert webcast on industrial system and recommendation integration is also planned for late summer.

We look forward to your questions and comments. Thanks for reading.

Written by Sonam Kinger & Kate Dowle

entry.title: Live Tax E-Reporting in SAP DRC - Digital Reporting Requirement in Europe - ViDA Peppol
entry.content:
Dear all,

I would like to know if e-reporting functionalities linked to Peppol invoicing and tax reporting are already integrated with SAP DRC (For Belgium and France) ? Deadline seems to be 2028... "  Any information or Roadmap about This would be helpfull...

From January 2028, Belgium is aiming for a 5-corner Peppol-model. As well as e-invoicing, this will include near-live e-reporting of transaction data (based on e-invoices) to replace the annual customer listing report. The aim is to help reduce the VAT Gap, the difference between forecast and actually collected tax. The latest Belgian VAT Gap is estimated by the EU to be €4.8bn per annum."

entry.title: Re: Previous year's total cannot be displayed in SAC reports
entry.content:
Ok, I see. I think it's not possible with restricted or calculated measure. I tried some functions like YTD, To Period or Iterate with no succeed. 

When you use the previous year on your restricted measure, SAC will find your data on 2024 in your model and will display on the time hierarchy of 2024, not 2025.

I recommand you to use a data action to copy your 2024 data on 2025. 

Best regards, 

Cynthia

entry.title: Re: DMEEX modification on existing format tree
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

When a DMEEX tree has a parent tree, the nodes from the parent tree can’t be deleted. It should be only a small disadvantage – and a big advantage of this limitation should be a possibility to synchronize changes from the parent tree to you child tree. When you do not want to have such nodes in your output file (so when the file is generated), you can deactivate them. And when you even do not want to see them in the tree (so when you work with the DMEEX transaction), you can hide deactivated nodes.

You can use context menu to Activate/Deactivate nodes or the penultimate button in the bar. And you can you can use the last button in the bar to display/hide deactivated nodes.

Best regards,

Antonin

entry.title: Re: Custom entity, $count, $skip, $top not supported
entry.content:
How about removing this?

@Aggregation.default           : #SUM

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Management System (DMS) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and On Premise
entry.content:
Hi Mates,

i've got a question about DMS and object links.

I would like to better understand which kind of information could be added through a custom document template with a view to enhancing the Document Info Records with fields from various tables (basic/master data and transactional / analytical).

(source : https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-document-management-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition/using-the-document-info-record)

(source: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/1317b7d3a93c4763a041b49532666fd7/2d0fc4528342494ee10000000a423f68.html)

In order words, does the DMS feature capable of getting a kind of report view to follow the status with the related (customized) data, not only attachment.

Thanks.

entry.title: Custom task with HTML Links
entry.content:
Hi,

We are using ONB and would like to check an option to use custom task with HTML Links.

Do we need to activate any provisioning switch for the same? 

Don't see any option in custom task. We can do MDF object and add a link in Manage Configuration UI, that's alternative option.

Thanks,

Ashish B

entry.title: Re: Difference b/w Planning Strategy 50 nd 52 . and cane u pl give brief explanation of 52 strategy
entry.content:
Hello @Raja_S_Reddy_B,

In Strategy 50, unrestricted stock is not considered during the MRP run, but in Strategy 52, unrestricted stock is considered during the MRP run

Feature Strategy 50 Strategy 52

Planning TypePlanned components, unplanned final productNo planning (pure MTO)
PIRs UsedYes, for components onlyNo PIRs at all
Final AssemblyTriggered by sales orderTriggered by sales order
Subassembly ProcurementCan be pre-plannedOnly after sales order
Finished Product StockNot created before sales orderNot created before sales order
Unrestricted Stock Considered in MRP?❌ No – Unrestricted stock is ignored during MRP run✅ Yes – Unrestricted stock is considered in MRP run
FlexibilitySemi-flexible (hybrid MTO)Fully customer-specific

entry.title: Re: DMEEX modification on existing format tree
entry.content: And you should use a numeric variable and a calculation node to allow the autonumbering

entry.title: Re: Flow accounting documents costs to material valuation...
entry.content:
Hello Heitor,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), posting accounting documents affecting material valuation is limited without Internal Orders, CO Production Orders, or WBS Elements. As an alternative, you can explore Material Ledger or Indirect Cost Allocation.

Given that Production Orders are planned for a later phase, you can leverage Inventory Valuation methods such as Moving Average Price (MAP) or Standard Price (SP) with Automatic Account Determination for accurate cost posting.

You can use Fiori apps or BTP extensions to adjust material stock directly for custom solutions.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SFTP Error on Report Schedule -Invalid credentials were provided. Password authentiation failed
entry.content:
Hi everyone,


I'm encountering an issue when scheduling a report to be delivered via SFTP in the 4.3 version. I have one report successfully sending to the SFTP server, but another report with the same configuration fails with the following error:
[login error. [Invalid credentials were provided. Password authentication failed.]: [CrystalEnterprise.Sftp]]
Here’s what I’ve checked so far:

  • Same SFTP host, port, username, and password are used for both reports
  • The working and failing reports are scheduled under the same user account
  • The destination settings in both reports appear identical

Does anyone know what could be causing this issue for one report but not the other?
Any insights or documentation references would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital payments add-on on S4/HANA RISE PCE & Thi...
entry.content:
Whether digital payment is required depends on the specific business process and the associated PCI (Payment Card Industry) requirements. It is important to note that S/4HANA Private Cloud does not natively provide PCI compliance. Therefore, SAP requires customers to utilize SAP Digital Payments Add-On to meet PCI attestation requirements.

That said, the final determination of PCI relevance for a customer's process design lies with a qualified PCI assessor. The assessor is responsible for evaluating the customer's specific process design to determine whether PCI attestation is required and achievable.

If the process is assessed as PCI-relevant, the SAP Digital Payments Add-On delivers the required services to help customers achieve PCI attestation.

In summary: While it is technically possible to process credit card payments in S/4HANA Private Cloud without using the SAP Digital Payments Add-On, it is required for PCI-relevant use cases to achieve PCI attestation.

I hope that helps!

entry.title: Error while deploying SAP Business One Client. Error code is 1106.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

If you're encountering issues with SAP CCC 1.1 PL 18 or PL 18 HF1, the root cause could be related to the SLD Agent service.

In my scenario, CCC was installed on a Windows Server, while the HANA database was hosted on a Linux server. The SLD Agent was installed on both servers. While creating the Common database from the Windows Server (as per the SAP B1 Cloud Administrator Guide), the SLD Agent successfully created the entry, but the process became stuck—the “Progress” column showed 100%, and the associated PID did not release.

This caused a deployment failure of the presentation server to the service units, returning the error:
“Error while deploying SAP Business One Client. Error code is 1106”

Eventually, I resolved the issue by uninstalling and reinstalling the SLD Agent (which took approximately 5 minutes). After that, the deployment of the SAP B1 Client Agent, Client, and DI API services completed successfully for the CCC environment.

Alternatively, as per SAP Note 2436005 – Registering Presentation Server Fails, if you do not wish to uninstall the SLD Agent, you can avoid this issue by installign the SAP services manually on the Windows Server, where the Common database was originally created.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking Trade Spend Control Efficiency with Funds Management in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content:
Thank you Karen - this is great blog to help understand the RGM Funds design and processes!

A question I have is that the Food & Beverage manufactures typically will have a Foodservice business that will sell commercially to distributors for restaurant/cafeteria business 9operators).  These agreements with the distributors and operators will be multi-year contracts (typically 3-years), so will RGM promotions be allowed to cross fiscal years and can the funds be setup for multi-years in the future?

If RGM can support more of the Foodservice processes, then RGM will have a competitive advantage over current TPM solution.

Thanks!

Steve

entry.title: Trigger event and Receive event don't work in Data Variables Logic Canvas
entry.content:
Hello @Dan_Wroblewski,

Can you help me?
I want to use the "Receive event" logic on a Data Variable canvas to trigger it from another part of the app, I set "Fired from Trigger Event" option in the event source, on the "Trigger event" I set it to trigger the receive event from earlier, but it doesn't show on the option list, instead I need to edit the trigger node and set the receive event there, but even that way the receive event doesn't get triggered, it only triggers events outside the Data Variables canvas.

I don't know if it makes sense but I add some screenshots to show how I set up.

Am I doing something wrong? How to properly set the events?

entry.title: Can ABLDT post in a special periods?
entry.content: During a Merger with Fixed Asset migration we need to post Asset values in a special period. ABLDT does not offer an input field for the posting period. We need to post in period 14.  Can anyone help? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Sales org specific number range for Settlement Docume...
entry.content:
Hi @kirankumardara, 

In standard configuration, you can determine the Number Range based on the Company code or Region, but not based on Sales Organization. 
Via Settlement Management>Basic Settings>Define Country/Region and Company Code Specific Number Ranges 
And assign the correct Number Range group into the Settlement document type. 

If the above doesn't suffice, you will have to explore the options via Transfer Manager and maybe create a Source Class to determine the Number range to be used based on the Sales Organization. 

Edit: Just saw you mentioned Public Cloud, I don't recall if any of the options above are available via configuration in SAP Public Cloud, you'll have to check. 

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: Business Role Groups & Business User Groups
entry.content:
You are right, there is no link and also no logic behind. I already wrote this but somehow it was not accepted as solution yet. They basically are the replacement for the former BRGRU which could be used to group and give access (via authorisations) to multiple objects at once. 

@kavya11 here you are 🙂

So is there any other chance to switch it to "Mile"? Maybe any company setting, business rule or whatever?

Kind regards

André

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2

entry.title: Get an order from SAP Customer Checkout Manager API
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have this scenario:

We are planning the implementation of Mobile Order in a customer. All the receipts created by this functionality will be replicated to an external system (following the steps mentioned in this blog). In addition to the receipt information, I need to gather the order information (for example, the assortment and location) because depending on this location we need to affect the stock availability of the products in the backend system (SAP Business One).

Checking the response of the endpoint mobilecheckout/orders I see this information is gathered there, but the endpoint needs the "orderUuid", and this is the internal code of the record in the CCOM database.

How is it supposed can I get the orderUuid to query the orders of the receipt?

The only field I see common between the receipt and the order is the "pickupId" (this is the ID used to pick the order up in the assortment) but this is not used in the endpoint as a parameter.

I appreciate your thoughts and how can I get the data I need for this business scenario.

Thanks you so much for your help.

entry.title: Re: Work permit alert expiration rule issue
entry.content: Any luck on this? I configured two rules, one for 3months, one for 6 weeks but only the 3 month one is triggering.

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community
entry.content:
Global Running Day with SAP Community – My 5K from Chennai!

On this Global Running Day – 4th June 2025 – I was thrilled to join fellow SAP enthusiasts across the world in celebrating wellness, energy, and community spirit. 🌍👟

I completed a 5 km run virtually from Chennai, India, and it felt great to be part of this global wave of positivity and motivation. Staying active is not just good for the body but also for the mind — especially for professionals like us balancing screens and systems!

🔹 Running improves Health!
🔹 SAP improves Efficiency!
🔹 SAP Community improves Knowledge!

As someone actively working with SAP Analytics Cloud, I use it daily to turn complex data into smart, insightful stories. It helps me and my team make faster and better decisions.

My journey with the SAP Community has been equally energizing — it’s a great platform to learn, share, and connect with like-minded professionals. Thank you to everyone who makes this community vibrant and supportive!

I’m happy to share a few photos from my Chennai run as part of this global celebration. Hope they inspire others to stay active and stay connected!

 

 

 

A big thanks to the organizers for bringing us together in such a fun and meaningful way. Looking forward to more such initiatives — and of course, more stories and smiles from around the world!

#SAPCommunity #GlobalRunningDay #SAC #SAPAnalyticsCloud #ChennaiRun #RunWithSAP

entry.content:
Hi Community,

I initially built my Web Intelligence report using a vertical table, but the GL Posting Month values appeared in rows. To get months in columns, I switched to a cross table, but now I'm not seeing proper column headers for Variance, Sales Quantity, and ECV Quantity.

My goal is to:

  • Display Posting Month across columns.
  • Show Variance, Sales Qty, and ECV Qty as separate column headers.

    Screenshots attached. Would appreciate any help on fixing the layout!

    Source for report:BexQuery

    Thanks!

entry.title: Encontro SAP - Let's talk about Joule (meetup)
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in São Paulo has a chance to meet after our SAP CodeJam and the night before the São Paulo SIT!

Encontro SAP in São Paulo will happen on July 25th, 2024 at 18:00 local time for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam event or the SIT, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers and technical conversations.

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", the location is a restaurant called Soul Botequim.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates Latin America Roadshow

entry.title: Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) can we consider all the records in Job information?
entry.content:
In SF-EC,Is there any way for Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) we can consider all the records in Job information. Based on the business rule/ eligibility rule its only considering the top record.

Scenario is we need to enable salary advance for people who went on deputation. Its considering only if it a top record. If at all any action is passed on top of the deputation action, then that eligibility is not getting considered.

entry.title: Report TIME in minutes using Story Reports
entry.content: Is there a way to adapt the Story Report Template Time Account Snapshot to report in MINUTES?  It's currently configured to show days and hours.

entry.title: Re: Creating a Sunburst Chart in SAP Analytics Cloud Using R and Plotly
entry.content: The chart looks great. Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: How to ensure AIco SOs are only created after SO approval, rather than upon ATP check confirmation?
entry.content:
How to ensure Intercompany Purchase and Sales Orders are only created after Sales Order approval, rather than upon ATP check confirmation? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to ensure AIco SOs are only created after SO appr...
entry.content:
• This exact functionality currently is not available, hence for submitting new feature requests you need to follow the correct channel and steps. These are outlined in SAP KBA 2963059 
  • As a workaround, it is possible to configure the Approval Reason with the setting "Set Confirmed Quantities to Zero" with SSC UI 102752.
  • Workaround:
      1. Ensure that Advanced Intercompany process is correctly configured on the system. 
      2. Open the Configuration Element "Define Reasons for Approval Requests" (SSCUI 102751).
      3. Create the following entries;
        Approval Request Reason ID: Z0R1
        Approval Request Reason: Sales Order Approval Request Reason
        External Workflow:
        Defer Purchase Requisition:
        Skip Approval Workflow:

        Approval Request Reason ID: Z0R2
        Approval Request Reason: Sales Order Approval w/o Confirmation
        External Workflow:
        Defer Purchase Requisition:
        Skip Approval Workflow:

      4. Open the Configuration Element "Assign Reasons for Approval Requests" (SSCUI 102752).
      5. Create the following entries;

        SD Document Category: C Order
        Approval Request Reason ID: ZOR1 Sales Order Approval Request Reason
        External Workflow:
        Defer Purchase Requisition:
        Set Confirmed Quantities to Zero:
        Skip Approval Workflow:

        SD Document Category: C Order
        Approval Request Reason ID: ZOR2 Sales Order Approval w/o Confirmation
        External Workflow:
        Defer Purchase Requisition:
        Set Confirmed Quantities to Zero: X (Set checkmark)
        Skip Approval Workflow:

      6. It is required to implement the BADI for the Approval Process (Cloud BAdI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON) to work correctly. This will need to be created by a developer to cater for the clients custom needs.
        Below is a sample coding snippet which could be included within the overall coding structure:

        **Testing SD for Advanced Intercompany Sales
        IF salesdocument-sddocumentcategory = 'C' AND salesdocument-salesdocumenttype = 'TA' AND salesdocument-sddocumentreason = '001'.
          salesdocapprovalreason = 'ZOR1'.
        ENDIF.

        IF salesdocument-sddocumentcategory = 'C' AND salesdocument-salesdocumenttype = 'TA' AND salesdocument-sddocumentreason = '002'.
          salesdocapprovalreason = 'ZOR2'.
        ENDIF.

      7. Create a Sales Order meeting the criteria set out above in terms of the Approval parameters.
      8. This should allow for the items not being confirmed and the hence follow up documents Inter Company Purchase Order & Intercompany Sales Order are not created.

entry.title: Pricing of SAP IAG
entry.content:
Hi All, 

there are 2 conflicting theories as provided by SAP. One is through below link, where blocks of 1000 user determines the price

https://store.sap.com/dcp/en/configure/vc/product/entityKey/000000000008900017?action=ORDER_NEW&product=8015452&productName=SAP%20Cloud%20Identity%20Access%20Governance&requiredRegistration=b2b&LIC_READONLY_ATTRIBUTES=LIC_RENEWAL_TYPE,LIC_EDITION&LIC_HIDE_ATTRIBUTES=LIC_EU_ACCESS&LIC_START_DATE=TODAY&LIC_EDITION=STD&LIC_DURATION_SEL=12&LIC_TRANSACTION_TYPE=ORDER_NEW&LIC_RENEWAL_TYPE=AUTO

The other theory as per SAP Note 3262845 - How to monitor IAG usage? says about Unique ids and Integration

Which among these is correct

regards

Plaban

Hi

is there someone that know how to replace a value in a column (AAA)  based a value of another column (BBB) ? keep in mind that I would like to substitute any valued in AAA column with (example) 1111 based a value in another .

entry.title: Re: List Report with OData V4: totals not supported?
entry.content:
same here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rap-view-entity-analytical-table-with-subtotals-totals-in-list-report-sap/qaq-p/14118877

Did you find a solution?

entry.title: Earned Leave accrual calculation based on Paid Days - Time Off
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
 
Requirement : Employees are entitled to 30 days of EL, given as monthly frequency 2.5 days. Monthly calculation is based on Paid days in a month which includes working days, holidays and week offs.
 
Calculation Logic : ELs = {(no. of paid days in a month x 2.5 ELs)/total no. of days in a month}
 
Scenario : If in May, the employee has 3 LWPs, 2.25 EL will be credited for that month, although if the employee then regularizes these LWP into a paid leave or missed punch, then in June month, he should get the deducted EL(0.25) from May month. Hence in June, he accrues 2.5+0.25=2.75 EL.
 
Please let me know, in case anyone has encountered a similar requirement.

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content:
Daniel.!

Your blog is very interesting and eductional. Thank you for sharing your experiences and impresions.

I love hearing stories from people as brave and capable as you. 

I'm just getting started with SAP, and in a few years, I'd like to continue sharing your passion for SAP.

Solo puedo decir  dos palabras "im presionante". Espero   que esta ultima frase te saque una sonrisa. 

Gracias de nuevo.

entry.title: Re: Error triggering an event from ECC to SAP Event Mesh
entry.content: Thank you. After performing the above issue is resolved. Now we see error " error uploading the sales order 401 unauthorized " meaning it is connecting to Event mesh and failing due to credentials ? how do we debug this ...

entry.title: Open PO Migration from ECC to S/4 integrated with SCC
entry.content: Hi All,  We have ECC system integrated with Ariba SCC currently and will be replacing ECC with S/4. The issue we're trying to figure out is let's say ECC PO has 5 line items (10, 20, 30, 40, 50) and while doing the open PO conversion from ECC to S/4, only line items 30 & 40 are truly open. When such PO gets migrated to from ECC to S/4, it will come with only two line items & SAP will renumber line item 30 & 40 to 10 & 20 respectively as SAP does not accept external item numbers and will regenerate them automatically. In such a case, I am afraid there will be integration errors (OC, ASN, GR, Invoice etc.) between S/4 & SCC is a question as Supplier may have 5 lines (original PO from legacy ECC) on Ariba network but now migrated S/4 PO will have only 2 lines with different line item numbers. Does anyone have any insights into how should we approach the open PO conversion to avoid issues? Any inputs will be highly appreciated. Thanks!

entry.title: Decimal places in currency settings
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

 

We are currently facing an issue when uploading bank statement files into the system. The upload fails with an error message indicating that the amount is not correct. However, if we remove the decimal places from the file, the issue no longer occurs.

Upon investigation, we observed that the issue is related to the currency settings: 2 out of 5 currencies in our system are currently set with 0 decimal places, while the others use the standard 2 decimal places.

 

Our Requirement:

We would like to standardize all currencies to use 2 decimal places to ensure consistency and to avoid errors during bank statement uploads and related processes.

Question:

How can we update the currency settings in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud so that all currencies are set to 2 decimal places?

We understand that currency decimal settings are typically predefined in the system. If it is not possible to change them directly, is there any recommended workaround or SAP-supported approach to resolve this issue?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

Gohar

entry.title: Re: Can't select 'Standard Hierarchy Node' in the App Manage Profit Centers
entry.content: Dear Colleague, I hope you find yourself well! Thank you for joining the community. As soon as possible, a technical expert will contact you to clarify your question. I am adjusting the tags to redirect the issue to the right team. Best Regards, Erick Goulardt SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Possibility to register different branches within one...
entry.content: Hello Kerisha, I found deocumentation that says it can be achived for both, customers and suppliers: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/a67653361d4549698d31490585b38423.html?locale=en-US&q=multiple+address+supplier, but direct documentation is as you said for customer, so it looks a bit tricky from documentation side. You can also serach for an API, that migth help you : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/f69dc6a7cd2f418f9ae309b2906f2c57.html?locale=en-US&q=multiple+address+supplier (please look at all folders, they're named Client, but docu there refers also to supplier). If non of these solutions fulfills your requirements you can think about creating your own enhancement or rising an Idea for enhancement on SAP website. Regards, Jagoda

entry.title: Re: gCTS in S/4HANA Cloud 2502: “Could Not Verify Content” / “Registered Commits couldn’t be read” E
entry.content:
We are facing a similar situation. Did you solve it?

When we add the repository, the gCTS App shows the Commits correctly, just like they have been pushed to Git.
We even tried to debug method check_before_import from the mentioned BADI. Apparently it tries to get the Commits from a service at https://userapps.support.sap.com/odata/gcts_commit_reg/CommitSet?$filter=Repository eq '[REPOSITORY_HASH]'. In our case this GET method returns with http status code 200, but the contents of the returned method are empty (in your case, I think you are getting a different http code, so your message is slightly different, but still around the same code block within the mentioned method).

We don't have access to a version 2408 system, so I can't tell if our scenario would work there. We will try to open a support ticket with SAP to see if they can help.

entry.title: Re: Pricing of SAP IAG
entry.content: I am not sure anymore if they sell the integration one, but I believe it's a version that you can only buy when combined with GRC On-Premise.

entry.title: SAP CPQ - Quote Migration Tool || Useful Tips for moving to Quote 2.0 Engine||
entry.content:
SAP CPQ Migration Guide to Quote 2.0 Engine

This blog serves you to get instructions and tips to convert your Quote 1.0 engine to Quote 2.0 engine quickly and efficiently using the Quote Migration tool.

Quote Migration Tool

Quote Migration Tool is self-service feature that is used for migration of Quote transactional data from Quote 1.0 engine to Quote 2.0 engine.

There are two migration processes that are executed with Quote Migration Tool:

1. Migrating Customers from Quote 1.0 to Business Partners in Quote 2.0.

2. Migrating quotes from Quote 1.0 to the Quote 2.0 environment.

Refer more detail in SAP CPQ Help Documentation - Quote Migration Tool

Pre-requisities

  • Customer should request a Production Database copy to Lower Environment to start Quote Migration - Component - CEC-SAL-CPQ
  • Customer should request to activate Quote 2.0 engine after the database copy 
  • Customer should request to enable Quote Migration Tool feature first in lower environment
    Refereable : Development or Test Environment
  • Customer should raise a SAP CPQ Support Ticket to enable Quote Migration Tool - Component - CEC-SAL-CPQ-Q2M
  • Customer to request for enabling Country/Region ISO code with 3 character  - This is under Feature Flag

Note : Known observation - There will be slowness during the Job running on Quote Migration Tool if there is no filter is selected for the Job.

Instructions for Quote Migration Tool 

Mapping of Fields

Ensure you map the right fields each by each as per the dropdown fields. Once migrated, you cannot re-run to correct the records.

Country/Region - Feature flag should be requested from SAP CPQ Support Ticket before running the job

Migrate ( 2 objects )

1. Migrating Customers from Quote 1.0 to Business Partners in Quote 2.0.

Turn off the toggle 'Migrate customers to Involved Parties' as shown in the screenshot (without any filter)

2. Migrating quotes from Quote 1.0 to the Quote 2.0 environment.

Turn on the toggle 'Migrate customers to Involved Parties' to migrate quotes with assigned Involved Parties. Use the filter option to run the job based on Status, Dates, or any other valid field, allowing for a quick validation with a few sample quotes.

Jobs

You can find the status of the job and track the progress of records that have run successfully. The difference represents the errors, which you can download to review in detail

Migrated Job (Actions)

you have options to Rollback the Job, View Filters of the current Job you have selected and Restart the Job if needed

Export to Excel 

You can download the job in Excel format to validate the number of quotes that were successfully migrated or encountered errors. The status will also display error records along with messages to help fix any issues.

Conclusion

The move to Quote 2.0 is more than an upgrade—it's a strategic shift toward greater efficiency and innovation. As new features continue to roll out, customers, services and partners will benefit from a more powerful quoting engine that meets the demands of a dynamic market.

Special Thanks

Special thanks to the CPQ Product Manager particularly Nikola Rakic for invaluable support and guidance on Quote Migration Tool!

entry.title: Re: How to use the Maintain Business Role Groups functionality
entry.content:
Hi,

We have been looking into business user groups and business role groups in SAP Public Cloud. Both don't do what I would have expected of it. You should be able to define role group that has a logical grouping of roles. Then create a User Group for users needing this group of roles and link the two. 

Then all you need to do is assign a new user to a user group and he/she will get all the roles from the role groups assigned to the business user group.

Unfortunately this is not possible and you still have to assign individual roles to users. To make this less labor intensive you end up creating large roles, which is very rigid.

You can also not copy a role and make changes, nor copy a user with all roles assigned. 

Sofar this concept looks many steps backwards from what you can do in ECC role/user management and way more work.

Eric

Have you tried this API:

https://api.sap.com/api/API_PURCHASING_SOURCE_SRV/overview

Please also check the app Maintain changes to source list, especially simulate source determination option:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/877fb65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

If it won't fit your requirements, thare is also possibility to create your own job template:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/1f04ad22db0147b99ebc476708b749b6.html?locale=en-US

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Report TIME in minutes using Story Reports
entry.content:
this standard template just do a SUM on collumn from EmployeeTime table.

Sum of quantity in Hours from TIMEMANAGEMENT#EmployeeTime#quantityInHours

Sum of Quantity in Days from TIMEMANAGEMENT#EmployeeTime#quantityInDays

if you need to convert in minutes you can create a calculation in the query to devide by 60 the TIMEMANAGEMENT#EmployeeTime#quantityInHours

for this, go to edit datasource, select under data on top collumn overview, select the tab calculation and create the following calculation: [Time Management#Employee Time#Total Number Of Hours]/60

if you really want to have only minutes from EmployeeTime table, you may want to post this to Employee time team if this is feasible to configure it in minutes instead of hours or get an additionnal collumn into employeetime table as minutes.

entry.title: Re: How to get row values as column heading in webi report
entry.content: you can add a second/blank row in header and edit these texts

hey @christof_mueller, thank you for the blog.

Can they use APRS or only ODP/ODQ/DataFlows(DI) and therefore not being capable of utilizing the object store? Also, can the user run transformation flows on the DPG LocalTable(File) / DeltaTables from BW?

cheers,
Sebas

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content: @albertosimeoni BDC  Current delta-share is enabled with SAP databricks only and for native databricks it's on the roadmap.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
@SreekanthSurampally 

if customer wants to use the custom data integration from S/4 Private cloud to Datasphere, is it possible in BDC?->YES

Create a Data product in SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: About 1-5 times per week our HA SQLA 17 database will begin pausing
entry.content:
So, here is a thought.

The DB is synchronized when the alert about the DB being behind is logged. This indicates that the log file is up to date. If it said 'synchronizing,' that means the log is not in sync.  

 
The Mirror behind alert indicates that the DB files/spaces are behind (i.e., not updated from the log file). The log file is not behind, as that should be real-time due to the setting being synchronized. 
 
That is why being behind XX seconds does not change the fact that the mirror can take over; it just takes longer to come up because it will have more to process into the database.
 
So that shifts the focus for me at least. Logfile is current, just the database files/space are not.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
@AWummel 

  1. Is the Object Store, that contains the Data Lake Files, Part of SAP Datasphere? Is the Object Store Part of the Foundation Services? Are the Foundation Services Part of SAP Datasphere? FOS layer and Object Store are different but based on HDLFS
  2. Are Data Products only referring to the Data Lake Files in the Object Store, or do also activated Objects in SAP Datasphere or SAP Databricks count as Data Products? Please refer to learning.
  3. Are Insight Apps and Intelligent Applications labelling the same thing? YES
  4. Do Insight Apps contain Datasets and Workflows within SAP Databricks? Will this be part of future developments? Future apps will be AI powered aswell.
  5. In regards to SAP BW/4HANA PCE, does the Data Product Generator create objects in the Object Store as Data Lake Files or as activated Objects directly in SAP Datasphere? Will this be supported by Insight Apps? Datasphere object store. You can use into custom/extended intelligent applications.
  6. Is the SAP-managed Space within SAP Datasphere separate from the Foundation Services? Are there only the activated objects of i.e. Insight Apps inside that space? YES, its an ingestion space for Datasphere.
  7. Joule has access to everything inside of Business Data Cloud, in addition to any SAP Cloud System that is connected. Are these considered Part of SAP BDC? In which Frontends would Joule typically be used in? Are any of those considered Part of SAP BDC? Please look at a roadmap.
  8. I have seen the term Data Product Package mentioned a few times. Could Insight Apps be considered a form of Data Product Package? Is there a clear differentiation? Data packages is grouping of Data products and Intelligent applications includes Data products, Datasphere models and SAC story. 
  9. Assuming as a partner I could provide Data Products and Data Product Packages. Is it feasible to curate these Packages like Insight Apps from SAP but have them managed by a partner in a similar fashion? Data packages/Data Products and Intelligent Applications are different as mentioned above.
  10. Will Data Integration primarily take place through the Foundation Services going forward? Or will it be more common to connect directly to SAP Datasphere? Which role do the SAP Cloud Connector and Data Provisioning Agent play? All depends on the use cases for the customer. Recommendations would be consume data products if available else native integration.
  11. Is storage of physical data in Datasphere a common occurence going forward? Or will the data storage primarily take place via Data Lake Files in the Object Store? FOS layer uses HDLFS + Apache Spark, Datasphere has HANA memory, disk memory, HDLFs (optional).

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Proxy Transfoms ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hello, marcospera.

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, you can create transformations to get the XML.

  METHOD generate_xml.

Example:

    CALL TRANSFORMATION zdemo_xml

              SOURCE ecf = i_ecf

              RESULT XML r_xml.

 

  ENDMETHOD.

I hope you find it useful.

best regards

entry.title: Re: Group Self Intro

entry.title: Re: Hello from Rio de Janeiro!
entry.content: Great to meet you!  Sorry I will only be in Sâo Paulo this year.  Tchau

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup em São Paulo – Um Encontro Antes do SITSP!
entry.content:
Esta é a versão Brasileira do evento Encontro SAP - Let's talk about Joule (meetup / SAP Stammtisch) 

No clima do SAP CodeJam e na véspera do SAP Inside Track São Paulo, a comunidade SAP local vai se reunir para um bate-papo informal, relaxado e fora do escritório, no dia 25 de julho de 2025, às 18h.

***Local a ser definido em breve***

Esse encontro não é palestra, nem workshop — é conexão humana. Não importa se você vai participar do CodeJam ou do SITSP Functional edition ou Developer edition, se você é dev, curioso, consultor, ou simplesmente gosta de conversar sobre SAP, você está mais do que convidado!

A ideia é simples: sentar com a galera, falar sobre o que tem de novo no mundo SAP, trocar experiências de projetos, rir um pouco, compartilhar ideias e fazer networking real, de verdade, olho no olho.

Vamos escolher um local perto do SAP CodeJam ou do SIT — o importante é juntar a comunidade.

A consumação é por conta de cada um, mas a vontade de trocar ideia já é o suficiente para participar.

Confirme presença clicando em “I will attend” no link do evento.

Bora se encontrar, bater um papo e fortalecer a comunidade SAP em São Paulo!

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Request for Hamburg - ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
entry.content: happy to be back, although more in the RAP corner 😉

entry.title: Re: CoA is not printing automatically after Post Goods issue for delivery.
entry.content: COA you mean - Chart of Accounts you want to print in material document?

entry.title: Re: How to update or remove notification emails using the...
entry.content: Thanks Michael, that helped point me in the right direction. I was able to clear both the success and failure notifications.

entry.title: Re: How to update or remove notification emails using the...
entry.content:
Here's what I ended up using:

try {
	oInfoObject = (IInfoObject) oInfoObjects.get(i);

	SI_ID = oInfoObject.properties().getProperty("SI_ID").getValue().toString();
	SI_NAME = oInfoObject.properties().getProperty("SI_NAME").getValue().toString();
	System.out.println(SI_ID + ": " + SI_NAME);

	ISchedulingInfo schedulingInfo = (ISchedulingInfo)oInfoObject.getSchedulingInfo();
	INotifications schedulingNotifications = schedulingInfo.getNotifications();
	
	IDestinations notificationDestinations = null;
	
	try {
		notificationDestinations = schedulingNotifications.getDestinationsOnSuccess();
		notificationDestinations.clear();
		iStore.commit(oInfoObjects);
		System.out.println("Success Notifications Cleared");
	} catch (NullPointerException e) {
		System.out.println("Success Notification Exception");
	}
	
	try {
		notificationDestinations = schedulingNotifications.getDestinationsOnFailure();
		notificationDestinations.clear();
		iStore.commit(oInfoObjects);
		System.out.println("Failure Notifications Cleared");
	} catch (NullPointerException e) {
		System.out.println("Failure Notification Exception");
	}
} catch (NullPointerException e) {
	System.out.println("No info");
}

entry.title: TM Freight Order Worklist
entry.content:
Quick question regarding the freight order worklist.  What am I doing wrong when searching Source location 0020002322 and Destination location 0030000005. In my opinion, only Freight Orders with that Source and the Destination location should come up. 

However, freight orders that do not meet any of these search criteria are also displayed (red). Only the green marked Freight orders are correct.

 

Am I doing something wrong with the "=" filter; i.e. should I use something else to only display these two location numbers and drill down on the freight order statuses?

entry.title: Incorrect delivery status "complete" after partial delivery for consig fillup, ABC- storage location
entry.content:
I have implemented ABC storage location for main item category below are settings same for sub item category. Also are listed below the schedule line item category. it is consignment fill-up scenario

1. Upon partially delivering the order line item show as fully delivered

2. Main item is showing open qty 10 where it should be only 1 as that s the open qty. 

Main item category 

respective schedule line item category 

 

Sub Item category 

respective schedule line item category 

Order details below we can see delivered is short but status is complete

 

VA05 open qty issue

entry.title: Re: First Half 2025 Release: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors Learning?
entry.content:
I would like to echo @RZAlexander's comments -- xAPI/cmi5 support would be excellent!

We experienced some issues with session timeouts using SCORM content and it took quite a long time to resolve. It appears the LMS' interpretation of historical AICC standards for parsing exit behaviour calls results in issues with sessions, unless workarounds (i.e. manually excluding cmi.core.exit functionality by editing the relevant .js files) are performed by modifying SCORM wrappers (that otherwise work without issue in other LMS' our organization works with).

Looking forward to xAPI/cmi5 support - and the related documentation - improving the experience for administrators, learners, and content developers!

Some links for historical reference:

  • Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence
  • XAPI Support for SF Learning is coming - what will... - SAP Community

entry.title: Rescheduling of periodic job failed - Company Payment jobs being erased from Production System
entry.content:
Hello everyone, thank you in advance for your attention.

I am having problems in the productive SAP ECC environment related to payment jobs and calendars. I'm constantly scheduling jobs for company payments, which only run on working days, from Monday to Friday, once a day. Some of these jobs are being canceled in production with the following message:

Rescheduling of periodic job failed. Three reasons are possible:
- Restriction (e.g. 'Omit job') with factory calendar EU critical
- Unable to load factory calendar EU or too many public holidays
- Period.job, scheduled for particular day in month, cannot find new date
Invalid start time displayed. Job chain ended.
Unable to find date in calendar. (Is the calendar correct?)
Job canceled

After this log, the job is deleted from production, stopping for good and requiring the entire configuration process to be redone. Some of these jobs run for months, others stop within weeks. What I find strange is the fact that all the calendars used are SAP's own standards, such as EU, CN, NA and so on. Calendars valid until 2030.

Do any of you know how to fix this?
I've searched SAP notes and even another similar post, but I can't find anything to help me.

entry.title: nothing!
entry.content: 91. CluB. In.v.ite Code. is 7647613896147

entry.title: Re: How to send parameter values to SAC Data Action throu...

entry.title: Re: Hide URL parameters in CRM with Content Server
entry.content: did you get any solution for this, we are also looking for same

entry.title: Re: Additional Binding annotation not working for consumption valuehepdefinition
entry.content:
Hello, Prasenjitsbist

You can be guided by these examples. Honestly, there are many ways.

 

Forma 1

 

Forma 2

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: 'ZB_VALUES_MONAT', element: 'value_low'  }, useForValidation: true }]

 

Forma 2

then in the other view the following is added

I hope to see you helped

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Why is SAP Desktop Agen...
entry.content:
Hi Adam,

For Document Extraction, you would have multiple steps including handling of file, which would not be possible based on the current functionality to handle on a process level. You would only have the option to manage this through an automation.

entry.title: Re: Actions in SAP Build Process Automation -- Need to ca...
entry.content:
Hi Parth,

Thanks for sharing the information.

Basically you need to complete the full URL. What this means is:

1. The path that is left over for the calling of your odata service will go to the prefix (Check the full odata URL, insert the path leftover after the path added in your destination, and just before the entity set name in the prefix).

2. Custom Action created should start with "/" followed by your entity set name along with any URL parameters that need to get passed in the API call.

3. These parameters are then defined in the first section of your action call.

4. Anything passed in the body of the payload is defined in the second section (body).

Once all these parameters are defined, on testing you can check if the URL is built and being called as expected, and adjust it as required.

Do let me know in case you need any further pointers.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: How to provide visibility to all requisitions globally
entry.content:
Currently myself (a Super Admin) and the manager of global Talent Acquisition must proxy as a recruiter to see their requisitions.  We need the ability to view all requisitions globally even though we are not the recruiter for that position.

I've tried to edit Recruiting permissions via manage permission roles but  "View Job Requisitions" permission is not an available option.

Please provide the steps or a KBA to show me how to configure access to and the ability to run a report that contains all requisitions globally without having to proxy as another recruiter.

Thakes for sharing your knowledge.

entry.title: Re: Encontro SAP - Let's talk about Joule (meetup)
entry.content: Let's go....
